title: Tioman Island Boat Rental Costs and Guide 2026
html:
Chartering a boat in Tioman Island offers access to secluded coves and vibrant reefs, with prices for a half-day motorboat hire starting from around 20 OMR. Whether you depart from Kampong Genting or Juara Bay, BoatHire24 connects you with verified skippers and private vessels to suit your group. You can compare options and book a boat online for your 2026 escape.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Tioman Island?

Prices range from 20 OMR for a small motorboat half-day to 120 OMR for a crewed yacht for a full day. The cost varies by boat type, duration and season. For a shared snorkelling tour expect to pay around 15 OMR per person, while a private catamaran for six people costs from 80 OMR for a full day. Always check what is included, such as fuel and skipper fees.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Tioman Island?

For most small motorboats and sailing catamarans in Tioman Island, no licence is required because the operator supplies a skipper. If you wish to drive a private vessel yourself, you will need a valid international driving permit or a recognised boating licence. The specific requirements depend on the boat's engine power and length, so the operator will confirm exact paperwork when you book through BoatHire24.

Where to set off in Tioman Island

The main departure points are the jetty at Kampong Tekek, the larger terminal at Kampong Genting, and the sheltered beach at Juara Bay. Other popular launch spots include Mukut village on the south coast and the small pier near ABC Beach. Many boat tours also operate from the jetty at Kampong Air Batang, known for its lively backpacker strip.

Which boat to rent

  1. Speedboat (e.g. QuickSilver Activ 605 Open - 150 HP), ideal for reaching Coral Island in 20 minutes; seats up to 6 guests; from 25 OMR for a half day. You can see the specifications and availability on this speedboat listing.
  2. Local fishing boat, perfect for a relaxed inshore trip to Monkey Bay; seats 4, 5 guests; from 20 OMR for a half day.
  3. Sailing catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 38 or similar), comfortable for day sails around the marine park; accommodates up to 8 guests; from 90 OMR for a full day.
  4. Crewed yacht (e.g. Sunseeker or Azimut), luxury option for up to 10 guests with skipper and crew; from 120 OMR for a full day.
  5. Kayak or paddleboard, self-guided exploration of the shoreline; for 1, 2 persons; from 5 OMR per hour.

Cost breakdown for a day on the water

  • Shared snorkelling tour (three stops: Coral Island, Renggis Island, Marine Park): 15 OMR per person
  • Small motorboat (half-day, up to 5 guests, no crew): from 20 OMR
  • Catamaran (full-day, up to 8 guests, with skipper): from 80 OMR
  • Private crewed yacht (full-day, up to 10 guests, with meals): from 120 OMR

When to go: seasons and local tips

The best time to charter a boat in Tioman Island is from April to October, when the northeast monsoon weakens and the sea is calm. During the high season (July, August and Christmas, New Year) the wind is typically light and the visibility for diving is at its best.

In the dry months the sea around Juara Bay is glassy in the mornings, making it the best time to cross to the uninhabited islets of the Tulai and Candad islands. The marine park will be quieter if you avoid the weekends.

At a glance

  • Price band: 15, 120 OMR per trip
  • Peak months: July, August, December
  • Trip length: half‑day (4 h) or full‑day (8 h)
  • Capacity: 1, 10 guests per vessel
  • Currency: OMR (Omani Rial)
  • Licence: not required when skipper included

Questions about boat rental in Tioman Island

Can I rent a boat for a day without a skipper?

Most operators in Tioman Island only rent out boats with a skipper for safety and to comply with local marine park rules.

What is the best boat for snorkelling trips?

A speedboat is the most practical choice because it can cover the distance to popular snorkelling spots like Renggis and Coral Island quickly and safely.

How far is it from Tekek to the marine park in nautical miles?

The marine park zone around Tioman begins about 2 nautical miles offshore from Tekek jetty, a 10‑minute speedboat ride.

Are there any additional costs I should budget for?

Fuel, skipper fees and marine park entrance permits are sometimes extra, so check the listing details before confirming your booking.

BoatHire24 makes it simple to compare boat hire options across Tioman Island and beyond. Whether you are planning a family day out or a solo adventure, you can browse the listings and book with confidence. If you own a boat in Tioman, consider joining the marketplace to list your own vessel and reach travellers from around the world.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Tioman Island and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
